meta.title: IB Mathematics AA & AI Masterclass, SL & HL, Live Online with IA Coaching
meta.description: Live online IB Maths course for Analysis & Approaches and Applications & Interpretation, SL and HL: syllabus taught for ownership, paper-specific technique, GDC fluency and honest IA coaching within IB integrity rules. 8 one-hour classes a month, free demo first.
keywords: ib math aa sl online classes
meta.level: IB DP students (AA & AI, SL & HL) and MYP students heading to the DP
meta.commitment: 2 live one-hour classes per week + weekly past-paper work
meta.certification: Course completion certificate + documented paper-score progression

program_overview.title: IB Mathematics AA & AI Masterclass
program_overview.tagline: Your exact course, your exact level, taught to a 7.
program_overview.description: A full-Diploma live program matched to the student's actual IB course: Analysis & Approaches or Applications & Interpretation, at SL or HL. IB maths punishes generic tutoring, the two courses examine different things, AA runs a strictly non-calculator paper while AI assumes the GDC everywhere, HL adds an extended problem-solving paper, and the Internal Assessment is worth a fifth of the grade. This masterclass teaches the syllabus for genuine ownership, trains paper-specific technique on real past questions and mark schemes, builds GDC fluency as a scored skill, and coaches the Mathematical Exploration honestly, structure, criteria and critique, with the work staying the student's own under IB academic integrity rules.
what_makes_this_different: Course-exact teaching: AA and AI are different subjects here, as they are on exam day
Non-calculator craft for AA Paper 1 and GDC power-workflows for AI, each trained deliberately
The IA coached properly: topic selection against the actual criteria, milestones, mathematical depth and honest draft critique, never ghost-writing
HL Paper 3 stamina built through olympiad-flavoured problem work
Predicted-grade awareness: the arc aims at internal assessments too, because those set the number universities see
MYP-era gaps repaired inline instead of ignored
One dedicated mentor across the whole two-year arc
Honest course-fit advice, including 'stay at SL' when that is the truth

learning_path.phase_1: The shared core built to DP strength: algebra, functions and the toolkit both courses stand on, with MYP debt repaired
learning_path.phase_2: Course-specific depth: calculus and proof for AA, statistics, modelling and technology for AI, plus IA season, shepherded
learning_path.phase_3: Syllabus completion, paper craft by course and level, HL Paper 3 training and full past-paper cycles into the exam session

career_outcomes: An IB maths grade that holds up predicted grades and university offers
AA students: the calculus and proof maturity maths-heavy degrees expect
AI students: the statistical and modelling literacy business and social-science degrees actually use
An IA the student can defend line by line, because it is genuinely theirs

phase_1_foundation.title: PHASE 1: The Shared Core at DP Strength (Months 1-8)
phase_1_foundation.description: Both IB courses stand on the same five topic areas. This phase builds number & algebra and functions to Diploma strength, repairs MYP-era gaps inline, and establishes the retrieval and paper habits that a two-year syllabus demands.

month_1_2.title: Months 1-2: Diagnostic, Course Fit and Algebra at DP Pace

week_1_2.title: Diagnostic and Course Fit (AA vs AI, SL vs HL)
week_1_2.description: Full diagnostic: syllabus position, MYP-era gaps, GDC habits, and an honest course-fit read (AA vs AI, SL vs HL) with evidence
topics: Full syllabus-position diagnostic across the five IB topic areas
Locating MYP-era gaps in algebra and number
Current GDC habits and calculator setup review
Evidence-based AA vs AI course comparison
SL vs HL demand and workload honesty
Baseline record set up for later progress tracking
week_1_2.practice: Complete a short mixed diagnostic set and log every gap it exposes into a personal error log.

week_3_4.title: Exponents and Logarithms to DP Fluency
week_3_4.description: Exponents, logarithms and the laws that both courses assume, taught from meaning, drilled to fluency
topics: Laws of exponents from first meaning
Logarithms as the inverse of exponentials
Laws of logarithms and change of base
Fluency drills that both courses assume
week_3_4.practice: Work a fluency set converting between exponential and logarithmic form and solving equations without hesitation.

week_5_6.title: Sequences, Series and Financial Applications
week_5_6.description: Sequences and series: arithmetic, geometric, sigma notation and financial applications (an AI favourite)
topics: Sum to infinity and the idea of convergence
Compound interest and annuities (an AI favourite)
Financial modelling contexts on the GDC
week_5_6.practice: Solve a mixed set that pairs pure sequence questions with a compound-interest financial application.

week_7_8.title: Proof and Mathematical Writing Foundations
week_7_8.description: Proof and reasoning foundations: direct proof and mathematical writing habits (deeper for AA HL)
topics: Deeper proof demands for AA HL
Setting the standard for written working
week_7_8.practice: Write out two short direct proofs with full justification and correct notation.

month_3_5.title: Months 3-5: Functions, the DP's Load-Bearing Topic

week_9_12.title: Function Anatomy: Domain, Range, Composition, Inverses
week_9_12.description: Function anatomy: domain, range, composition and inverses, the material the whole DP silently assumes
topics: One-to-one functions and the horizontal-line idea
Why the whole DP silently assumes this fluency
week_9_12.practice: Build a reference sheet of domain, range and inverse for a set of standard functions.

week_13_16.title: Transformations, Quadratics and Polynomials
week_13_16.description: Transformations and graph literacy; quadratics and polynomials to DP depth; the discriminant as an idea
topics: Translations, stretches and reflections of graphs
Quadratics in vertex, root and factored forms
The discriminant as an idea, not just a formula
Polynomial behaviour and the factor theorem
week_13_16.practice: Sketch a family of transformed graphs by hand, then confirm each on the GDC.

week_17_20.title: Exponential, Log and Rational Functions with the GDC
week_17_20.description: Exponential, logarithmic and rational functions with modelling contexts; GDC graphing workflows introduced properly
topics: GDC graphing workflow set up properly
Interpreting features straight from a graph
week_17_20.practice: Model a given real context with an exponential or rational function and justify the fit on the GDC.

month_6_8.title: Months 6-8: Geometry, Trigonometry and First Paper Craft

week_21_24.title: Trigonometry: Unit Circle to Applied Bearings
week_21_24.description: Trigonometry rebuilt: unit circle, identities and equations (AA depth) / right-triangle and applied trig with bearings (AI emphasis)
topics: Unit circle and exact values (AA depth)
Applied trig with bearings and angles of elevation
week_21_24.practice: Complete a split set: AA exact-value and identity work, AI applied bearings problems.

week_25_28.title: Vectors, 3D Geometry and the Sine and Cosine Rules
week_25_28.description: Vectors (AA HL) and 3D geometry; sine and cosine rules in applied problems
topics: The sine rule in non-right triangles
The cosine rule and the ambiguous case
week_25_28.practice: Solve a set of non-right-triangle problems, choosing the sine or cosine rule deliberately.

week_29_32.title: First Past-Paper Season and the Review Ritual
week_29_32.description: First past-paper season: question anatomy, mark-scheme logic, and the review ritual used for the next eighteen months
topics: Anatomy of an IB exam question
Command terms and what they demand
The review ritual used for the next eighteen months
week_29_32.practice: Sit one short past-paper section under time, then self-mark it against the real mark scheme.
week_29_32.assessment: End-of-phase checkpoint: a timed past-paper section marked against the official scheme sets the Phase 1 baseline.

phase_2_course_depth.title: PHASE 2: Course-Specific Depth + the IA (Months 9-16)
phase_2_course_depth.description: The AA and AI tracks now genuinely diverge: calculus and proof depth for AA, statistics and modelling with full GDC fluency for AI. In the middle of this phase sits IA season, the Mathematical Exploration, coached against the real criteria while remaining the student's own work.

month_9_11.title: Months 9-11: The AA Track / The AI Track

week_33_36.title: AA Differentiation from First Principles / AI Statistics and Regression
week_33_36.description: AA: limits and differentiation from first principles; the derivative toolkit including chain rule mastery. AI: descriptive statistics, correlation and regression on the GDC
topics: AA: limits and the idea of a derivative
AA: derivative toolkit and chain rule mastery
AI: linear regression on the GDC
week_33_36.practice: AA students differentiate a mixed set including chain-rule cases; AI students run a regression on a dataset and interpret it.

week_37_40.title: AA Applications and Integration / AI Probability Distributions
week_37_40.description: AA: applications, tangents, optimisation, kinematics; introduction to integration. AI: probability, distributions (binomial, normal) and interpretation craft
topics: AI: binomial and normal distributions with interpretation
week_37_40.practice: AA students solve an optimisation and a kinematics problem; AI students compute and interpret binomial and normal probabilities.

week_41_44.title: AA Integration and Areas / AI Modelling Cycle and Networks
week_41_44.description: AA: integration techniques and areas/volumes. AI: modelling cycle, choose, fit, critique and improve a model; matrices and networks (HL)
topics: AA: definite integrals and area under a curve
AI: the modelling cycle, choose, fit, critique, improve
week_41_44.practice: AA students find an area and a volume by integration; AI students fit and critique a model for a given dataset.

month_12_14.title: Months 12-14: IA Season, the Exploration, Shepherded

week_45_48.title: IA Topic Selection and Proposal
week_45_48.description: Topic selection against the five criteria: personal engagement that is real, mathematics that can score; proposal locked with milestones
topics: The five IA assessment criteria explained
Mathematics chosen so it can actually score
A milestone plan for the IA
Academic integrity expectations set from the start
week_45_48.practice: Draft three candidate IA questions and test each honestly against the five criteria.
week_45_48.assessment: IA milestone: the exploration proposal is locked with a dated milestone plan.

week_49_52.title: Deepening the IA Mathematics
week_49_52.description: The mathematics deepened: the mentor pushes rigour and aim while the student drives; communication and notation standards set
topics: Pushing mathematical rigour in the exploration
Keeping the exploration honestly the student's own
week_49_52.practice: Develop the core mathematics of your exploration and annotate where each criterion is being met.

week_53_56.title: IA Draft Critique and Final Polish Plan
week_53_56.description: Draft critique rounds: honest feedback against each criterion, reflection strengthened, and the final polish plan, the work staying the student's own throughout, per IB academic integrity rules
week_53_56.practice: Revise your IA draft against criterion-by-criterion feedback and record the changes you make.
week_53_56.assessment: IA milestone: a full draft is reviewed against all five criteria before the final polish.

week_57_60.title: AA Further Calculus / AI Hypothesis Testing
week_57_60.description: AA: further calculus (HL: deeper techniques, Maclaurin ideas); AI: hypothesis testing, chi-squared and t-tests with interpretation
topics: AA HL: deeper techniques and Maclaurin ideas
AI: the logic of hypothesis testing
week_57_60.practice: AA students tackle a further-calculus set; AI students run and interpret a chi-squared or t-test.

week_61_64.title: HL Paper 3 Investigations Introduced / SL Consolidation
week_61_64.description: HL students of both courses: Paper 3-style extended investigations introduced; SL students: consolidation cycles by error-log priority
topics: HL: the structure of Paper 3 extended investigations
HL: harvesting earlier parts of a problem
SL: targeted retrieval of weak topics
Both: connecting topics across the syllabus
week_61_64.practice: HL students attempt one extended investigation; SL students clear their top three error-log items.
week_61_64.assessment: End-of-phase checkpoint: HL students are marked on a Paper 3 style investigation and SL students on a targeted consolidation set.

phase_3_exam_mastery.title: PHASE 3: Syllabus Close + Paper Craft to Exam Session (Months 17-24)
phase_3_exam_mastery.description: The remaining syllabus is closed with margin, then the program becomes exam craft: papers by course and level, calculator and non-calculator discipline, HL Paper 3 stamina, and full timed cycles that peak in the exam session, with predicted-grade checkpoints along the way.

month_17_19.title: Months 17-19: Closing the Syllabus with Margin

week_65_70.title: Closing the Final Syllabus Topics
week_65_70.description: Final syllabus topics per course and level, taught early enough to settle: remaining probability/statistics (AA), remaining calculus for AI HL, kinematics and applications
topics: Topics taught early enough to settle
Linking new topics back to earlier work
week_65_70.practice: Complete a set on the last new topics for your exact course and level.

week_71_76.title: Full-Syllabus Connection Map and Spaced Retrieval
week_71_76.description: Full-syllabus connection map built by the student; spaced retrieval cycles across all five topic areas
topics: Building a personal syllabus connection map
week_71_76.practice: Build a one-page connection map of the whole syllabus and schedule your spaced retrieval.

month_20_22.title: Months 20-22: Paper Craft by Course and Level

week_77_80.title: AA Paper 1 Non-Calculator Craft / AI GDC Speed
week_77_80.description: AA Paper 1 non-calculator craft: exact values, structure recognition and clean written working. AI: GDC speed workflows for every paper
topics: AA: clean written working for method marks
AI: efficient graphing, solving and regression
week_77_80.practice: AA students complete a non-calculator set; AI students time their GDC workflows on typical questions.

week_81_84.title: Paper 2 Technique and Time Allocation
week_81_84.description: Paper 2 technique: extended structured questions, method-mark discipline and time allocation from personal data
week_81_84.practice: Sit a Paper 2 section and log your time per question to set personal pacing targets.

week_85_88.title: HL Paper 3 Stamina and Investigation Craft
week_85_88.description: HL Paper 3: long unfamiliar investigations, harvesting earlier parts, keeping momentum, and the calm that stamina training buys
topics: Harvesting earlier parts to reach later ones
Managing time across a long problem
The calm that stamina training buys
week_85_88.practice: Complete a full HL Paper 3 style investigation under time and review it against the mark scheme.

month_23_24.title: Months 23-24: Full Cycles and the Exam Session

week_89_92.title: Full Timed Past-Paper Cycles
week_89_92.description: Full timed past-paper cycles by exact course and level, reviewed against real mark schemes; error-log closure
topics: Papers for the exact course and level
week_89_92.practice: Sit a full timed paper, self-mark it, and close the error-log items it reveals.
week_89_92.assessment: Mock checkpoint: full timed papers are marked against official schemes and score progression is recorded.

week_93_96.title: Peak Plan and the Exam Session
week_93_96.description: Peak plan: final consolidations, rest discipline, exam-day routines, and the session met as a rehearsed place
topics: Meeting the session as a rehearsed place
week_93_96.practice: Run a final light consolidation and rehearse your full exam-day routine.
week_93_96.assessment: The IB examination session: papers are sat for the student's exact course and level.

faqs.question: Do you teach both AA and AI, at both SL and HL?
faqs.answer: Yes, and separately, because they are genuinely different courses. The mentor teaches your student's exact registration: AA's algebraic, proof-flavoured, calculus-heavy road or AI's modelling, statistics and technology-first road, at the right level. The free demo doubles as a course-fit consult if the choice is still open.

faqs.question: How does the IA coaching work, and how far is too far?
faqs.answer: We coach the Mathematical Exploration properly: topic selection against the actual criteria, structure, pushing the mathematics deep enough to score, and honest critique of drafts. We do not write it or polish prose into someone else's voice, IB academic integrity rules are real, schools check, and the IA only helps your student if it is genuinely theirs. Done right, it is the most coachable 20% of the grade.

faqs.question: My AA student falls apart on the non-calculator paper. Fixable?
faqs.answer: Very. Paper 1 skill is mostly exact-value fluency plus structure recognition, seeing the quadratic inside the exponential equation. Both are trained, not innate, and every AA class includes non-calculator work so Paper 1 becomes routine long before the exam.

faqs.question: Do you teach GDC (graphing calculator) skills?
faqs.answer: As a first-class skill, because AI examines it and AA's Paper 2 rewards it: efficient graphing, solving and regression workflows, and knowing what counts as acceptable use of technology. AI students who underuse the GDC leave marks and minutes on the table.

faqs.question: Can you help before predicted grades are set?
faqs.answer: That is the highest-leverage timing in the IB, predictions ride on school assessments, so a focused arc aimed at the next internal exams moves the number universities actually see. Start earlier than feels necessary; predictions harden sooner than most families expect.

faqs.question: My child is in the MYP. Is this course relevant yet?
faqs.answer: Yes, MYP students join the Phase 1 material aimed at arriving in the DP with the algebra and functions base both courses silently assume. It is the cheapest IB maths insurance there is.

faqs.question: What does the course cost?
faqs.answer: Small-group classes are ₹1,499 per month and private 1-on-1 mentorship is ₹4,999 per month in India; international students pay USD $100 per month for group and $150 for 1-on-1, while IB-specialist agencies bill $60 to $120 per hour for the same format. 8 live one-hour classes a month, recordings included, free demo first.

faqs.question: Can we try it before paying?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every student starts with a free live demo class that doubles as the diagnostic and course-fit consult, no card details, no obligation.

course_guarantees.live_classes: Live, interactive classes with a real instructor, never pre-recorded videos.
course_guarantees.small_batches: Small batches only: group classes are capped at 10 students, with mini-batch (3 to 4 students) and personal 1-on-1 options.
course_guarantees.structured_curriculum: A structured, well-paced curriculum taught step by step, with hands-on practice in every session.
course_guarantees.doubt_support: Doubt support between classes over WhatsApp, so you are never left stuck.
course_guarantees.certificate: A course-completion certificate you can share.
course_guarantees.free_demo: A free demo class before you enrol, so you can decide with no pressure.
